shared_with.note: Kenya/Uganda use the same white-front/yellow-rear scheme.

hints.text: All **Gen4**; the pickup truck is usually covered by one **big blur** — sometimes fully visible: big white or brown pickup w/ brakelight + camera mount
Coverage has a noticeable **hazy tint**, esp. in the sky

shared_with.diff: Hills + red vests → Rwanda; flat sandy Sahel → Senegal.

hints.text: Brick & concrete single-family houses, often steep roofs + fences; signature **'ventilation bricks'** in many walls
hints.uniq_note: The ventilation-brick rows are the Rwandan architecture tell.

hints.text: Generally **hilly** (not truly mountainous); soil w/ a noticeable **red tint**; **eucalyptus** + **banana plants** everywhere
Multiple **red-white cylindrical bollards**; various **white concrete waystones**
Streets **remarkably clean**; kerbs painted **black-white**; villa areas w/ tall security walls; drives on the **right**
**All motorcyclists wear red vests & helmets** (required by law)
Coverage limited: **Kigali + a few patches** (still expanding)

Street names carry a **2-letter district code**; national-road lampposts have the **road number painted in black** + a lamp number that (usually) rises away from Kigali; lamp-arm style varies consistently by national road (map)

**Tea plantations → west**; NE = gentler rolling hills + open farmland/grassland; **volcanoes + volcanic rock → NW**; **Akagera NP** = dry but vegetated, wide reddish-brown unpaved roads, rectangular waystones, brown car

**Road spotlights**: Nyungwe Forest NP = lush hilly, NR10 paved w/ lines + French-style bollards + green lamps, NR26 mostly paved w/o lines (brown car); NR11 north of Mabanza = all-white lines; NR15 Muhanga→West border = potholed & lineless, then **unpaved along a red-dirt cliffside**; NR16 = the only road w/ predominantly dashed outer lines; wide river along NE Kigali; Kigali coverage = mostly rich villa areas; **Butare** = cobblestone streets
